metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_494
metadata.comment:
Rationale: CAPS-1 is essential for synaptic vesicle priming. Isolated, autaptic hippocampal neurons deficient for CAPS-1 as well as CAPS-1 and -2 exhibit reduced evoked neurotransmitter release, spontaneous release and readily-releasable pool size in response to hypertonic sucrose application, without a change in the vesicular release probability.

12/5/2017 Pim
- The "regulation of..." box not checked because CAPS1 is shown to be "Essential Synaptic Vesicle Priming Proteins" (see title of referenced paper).. Experimental description: M&M: Mutant Mouse Strains. CAPS-1 KO mice were published previously (Speidel et al., 2005). CAPS-2 KO mice were generated by homologous recombination in embryonic stem cells (Figure S1).
